caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 4-digit 5418 occupation mix is dominated by advertising occupations and is not a physical-distribution occupational census.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The residual's field-labor interpretation is a bounded judgment; a sampling-heavy target may be even less automatable.
inputs.pi_dist: EDDM is an alternative for some, not all, direct-distribution work; the source does not measure private-firm pass-through.
inputs.pi_moat: No public NAICS-specific retention or switching-cost study was located.
inputs.t50_years: S6 is adjacent printing research, not a 541870 adoption survey.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: Effective adoption excludes generic individual use of AI and does not imply delivery automation.
inputs.historical_analogs: No historical AI adoption dataset for 541870 was found.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: S7 is national small-business evidence, not a representative 541870 owner-age census.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: False means not documented in the reopened sources, not that succession risk is absent.
inputs.active_consolidators: A target search could identify buyers, but that would be new research rather than evidence for this initial count.
inputs.buy_mult: S4 is SDE/owner earnings, not EBITDA, and is neither exact NAICS 541870 nor direct-distribution transaction evidence.
inputs.exit_mult: This is an underwriting scenario based on a weak SDE/printing proxy, not a reported exit multiple.
inputs.pricing_structure: EDDM does not cover every non-postal distribution or in-store sampling assignment.
S2 supports a 99 billion FY2008 to 59 billion FY2023 Marketing Mail decline, not the memo's 80.9 billion FY2016 to 56.8 billion FY2025 endpoints.
The MELTING terminal classification is supported mainly by postal Marketing Mail evidence; non-postal flyers and in-store sampling within 541870 may have a different demand trajectory.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $291,195 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541870); class <5: 958 firms, avg revenue/firm $437,937 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $68,537 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 114 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,846,219 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $288,933 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 65 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,232,815 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $505,936 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 64 firms, avg revenue/firm $8,283,953 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,296,439 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 25 firms, avg revenue/firm $44,197,280 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $6,916,874 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 13 firms, avg revenue/firm $58,769,077 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $9,197,361 -> IN $1-10M band; n_band = 102

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: Only routing, targeting, sales support, reporting, administration and some supervision are AI-addressable. The exact industry definition is physical distribution/delivery, so the residual is intentionally assigned a low fraction to represent unlisted field labor.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541870 ($1,301,952,215) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541870 ($3,239,249,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: Mechanics pass. The low-confidence kill thesis remains useful and rubric-consistent: physical field labor limits V, rebiddable services limit C, and capture is kept distinct from terminal demand. Publication must retain all authored caveats plus the corrected mail-volume attribution and terminal-scope caveats.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Exact 2022 scope for 541870 physical distribution/delivery, and its boundary with 541860 direct-mail advertising, printing and advertising specialties.

sources.title: USPS OIG: Analysis of Historical Mail Volume Trends
sources.what_it_supports: USPS OIG evidence that Marketing Mail volume fell 40% from FY2008 to FY2023 because of diversion to lower-cost online alternatives.

sources.title: USPS Every Door Direct Mail
sources.what_it_supports: USPS EDDM offers neighborhood, city and ZIP Code targeting through retail or business-mail entry, a physical-distribution alternative relevant to this industry.

sources.title: Paper Manufacturing & Printing Business Valuation Benchmarks
sources.what_it_supports: Sold small printing-business owner-earnings/SDE valuation data: 2.45x median and 2.57x average for 2021-25; used only as a disclosed proxy, not as direct 541870 EBITDA evidence.

sources.title: USPS Postal Facts: Every Door Direct Mail
sources.what_it_supports: USPS reports nearly 3bn EDDM pieces sent in FY2025, showing the physical channel persists even though it does not prove distributor margins.

sources.title: AI Adoption in the Printing Industry: From Curiosity to Competitive Advantage
sources.what_it_supports: 2025 print-provider survey evidence on AI workflow use, fragmented adoption, barriers and the continued role of human staffing; used as a broad adjacent workflow proxy.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.what_it_supports: 2024 U.S. small-business-owner survey on sale, transfer, closure and succession plans; used only as a broad owner-succession proxy.

title: Advertising Material Distribution Services
